{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27629","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27629/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27629/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27629/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/27629","id":2609867979,"node_id":"I_kwDOAA3dP86bj2zL","number":27629,"title":"BUG: memory leak on `numpy.linalg.solve`","user":{"login":"HicaroD","id":75737377,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc1NzM3Mzc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/75737377?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/HicaroD","html_url":"https://github.com/HicaroD","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/HicaroD/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/HicaroD/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/HicaroD/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/HicaroD/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/HicaroD/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/HicaroD/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/HicaroD/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/HicaroD/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/HicaroD/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":6359214,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2MzU5MjE0","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/00%20-%20Bug","name":"00 - Bug","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-10-23T21:17:24Z","updated_at":"2024-10-23T21:17:24Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/54d173a2-42cf-405d-9e5f-76a5613fa5bd)

At my job, I was doing some memory analysis on our application in order to identify possible memory leaks.

To do this, I've used the package `memray`, which offers a lot of handy commands to identify memory leaks by generating a flame graphs that identifies memory chuncks allocated after tracking starts and not deallocated after tracking ends.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
# test_numpy_leak.py
import numpy as np


def main():
    for _ in range(1_000_000):
        a = np.array([[1, 2], [3, 5]])
        b = np.array([1, 2])
        np.linalg.solve(a, b)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()


In order to reproduce the analysis, use the following commands:
- `PYTHONMALLOC=malloc python -m memray run --native test_numpy_leak.py`
- `python -m memray flamegraph --leaks memray-test_numpy_leak.py.XXXX.bin`, where `XXXX` is the number of the file specified in its name.

After running these commands, it generates an HTML file, so you can open in your browser and see a flame graph, like the one above. I highlighted in red the section where `memray` reports a memory leak, if I hover it, it says that 4 allocations was made (32.0 MiB in total).

The line of code that is leaking memory is the following: https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/41c6bbe7ea2b4b16cdce99a75f3cac35e533bcef/numpy/linalg/_linalg.py#L410
```


### Error message:

_No response_

### Python and NumPy Versions:

1.26.2
3.11.9 (main, Jul 19 2024, 18:26:46) [GCC 14.1.1 20240522]

### Runtime Environment:

_No response_

> 
> It would be interesting to compile a debug build of Python so you can check that [`sys.gettotalrefcount()`](https://docs.python.org/3/using/configure.html#python-debug-build) isn't monotonically increasing as you loop over calling the gufunc over and over. If it is then it's likely there's a reference counting bug somewhere in NumPy's C internals.

Great point. I'll do it ASAP!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2433538767/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
